root_text.an10.87:1.1: Tatra kho bhagavā kālaṅkataṁ bhikkhuṁ ārabbha bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an10.87:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhikaraṇiko hoti, adhikaraṇasamathassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:2.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhikaraṇiko hoti adhikaraṇasamathassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na sikkhākāmo hoti, sikkhāsamādānassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:3.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na sikkhākāmo hoti sikkhāsamādānassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pāpiccho hoti, icchāvinayassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:4.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pāpiccho hoti icchāvinayassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kodhano hoti, kodhavinayassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:5.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kodhano hoti kodhavinayassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu makkhī hoti, makkhavinayassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:6.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu makkhī hoti makkhavinayassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saṭho hoti, sāṭheyyavinayassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:7.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saṭho hoti sāṭheyyavinayassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu māyāvī hoti, māyāvinayassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:8.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu māyāvī hoti māyāvinayassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:9.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammānaṁ na nisāmakajātiko hoti, dhammanisantiyā na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:9.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammānaṁ na nisāmakajātiko hoti dhammanisantiyā na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na paṭisallīno hoti, paṭisallānassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:10.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na paṭisallīno hoti paṭisallānassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:11.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ na paṭisanthārako hoti, paṭisanthārakassa na vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:11.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ na paṭisanthārako hoti paṭisanthārakassa na vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo na piyatāya na garutāya na bhāvanāya na sāmaññāya na ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:12.1: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno kiñcāpi evaṁ icchā uppajjeyya:
root_text.an10.87:12.2: ‘aho vata maṁ sabrahmacārī sakkareyyuṁ garuṁ kareyyuṁ māneyyuṁ pūjeyyun’ti, atha kho naṁ sabrahmacārī na ceva sakkaronti na garuṁ karonti na mānenti na pūjenti.
root_text.an10.87:12.4: Tathāhissa, bhikkhave, viññū sabrahmacārī te pāpake akusale dhamme appahīne samanupassanti.
root_text.an10.87:13.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, assakhaḷuṅkassa kiñcāpi evaṁ icchā uppajjeyya:
root_text.an10.87:13.2: ‘aho vata maṁ manussā ājānīyaṭṭhāne ṭhapeyyuṁ, ājānīyabhojanañca bhojeyyuṁ, ājānīyaparimajjanañca parimajjeyyun’ti, atha kho naṁ manussā na ceva ājānīyaṭṭhāne ṭhapenti na ca ājānīyabhojanaṁ bhojenti na ca ājānīyaparimajjanaṁ parimajjanti.
root_text.an10.87:13.4: Tathāhissa, bhikkhave, viññū manussā tāni sāṭheyyāni kūṭeyyāni jimheyyāni vaṅkeyyāni appahīnāni samanupassanti.
root_text.an10.87:13.5: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, evarūpassa bhikkhuno kiñcāpi evaṁ icchā uppajjeyya:
root_text.an10.87:13.6: ‘aho vata maṁ sabrahmacārī sakkareyyuṁ garuṁ kareyyuṁ māneyyuṁ pūjeyyun’ti, atha kho naṁ sabrahmacārī na ceva sakkaronti na garuṁ karonti na mānenti na pūjenti.
root_text.an10.87:13.8: Tathāhissa, bhikkhave, viññū sabrahmacārī te pāpake akusale dhamme appahīne samanupassanti.
root_text.an10.87:14.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na adhikaraṇiko hoti, adhikaraṇasamathassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:14.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na adhikaraṇiko hoti adhikaraṇasamathassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo piyatāya garutāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:15.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sikkhākāmo hoti, sikkhāsamādānassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:15.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sikkhākāmo hoti sikkhāsamādānassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo piyatāya garutāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:16.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu appiccho hoti, icchāvinayassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:16.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu appiccho hoti icchāvinayassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo …pe… ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:17.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkodhano hoti, kodhavinayassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:17.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkodhano hoti kodhavinayassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo …pe… ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:18.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu amakkhī hoti, makkhavinayassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:18.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu amakkhī hoti makkhavinayassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo …pe… ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:19.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu asaṭho hoti, sāṭheyyavinayassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:19.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu asaṭho hoti sāṭheyyavinayassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo …pe… ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:20.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu amāyāvī hoti, māyāvinayassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:20.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu amāyāvī hoti māyāvinayassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo …pe… ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:21.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammānaṁ nisāmakajātiko hoti, dhammanisantiyā vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:21.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammānaṁ nisāmakajātiko hoti dhammanisantiyā vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo …pe… ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:22.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭisallīno hoti, paṭisallānassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:22.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭisallīno hoti paṭisallānassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo …pe… ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:23.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ paṭisanthārako hoti, paṭisanthārakassa vaṇṇavādī.
root_text.an10.87:23.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ paṭisanthārako hoti paṭisanthārakassa vaṇṇavādī, ayampi dhammo piyatāya garutāya bhāvanāya sāmaññāya ekībhāvāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an10.87:24.1: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno kiñcāpi na evaṁ icchā uppajjeyya:
root_text.an10.87:24.2: ‘aho vata maṁ sabrahmacārī sakkareyyuṁ garuṁ kareyyuṁ māneyyuṁ pūjeyyun’ti, atha kho naṁ sabrahmacārī sakkaronti garuṁ karonti mānenti pūjenti.
root_text.an10.87:24.4: Tathāhissa, bhikkhave, viññū sabrahmacārī te pāpake akusale dhamme pahīne samanupassanti.
root_text.an10.87:25.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, bhaddassa assājānīyassa kiñcāpi na evaṁ icchā uppajjeyya:
root_text.an10.87:25.2: ‘aho vata maṁ manussā ājānīyaṭṭhāne ṭhapeyyuṁ, ājānīyabhojanañca bhojeyyuṁ, ājānīyaparimajjanañca parimajjeyyun’ti, atha kho naṁ manussā ājānīyaṭṭhāne ca ṭhapenti ājānīyabhojanañca bhojenti ājānīyaparimajjanañca parimajjanti.
root_text.an10.87:25.4: Tathāhissa, bhikkhave, viññū manussā tāni sāṭheyyāni kūṭeyyāni jimheyyāni vaṅkeyyāni pahīnāni samanupassanti.
root_text.an10.87:26.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, evarūpassa bhikkhuno kiñcāpi na evaṁ icchā uppajjeyya:
root_text.an10.87:26.2: ‘aho vata maṁ sabrahmacārī sakkareyyuṁ garuṁ kareyyuṁ māneyyuṁ pūjeyyun’ti, atha kho naṁ sabrahmacārī sakkaronti garuṁ karonti mānenti pūjenti.
root_text.an10.87:26.4: Tathāhissa, bhikkhave, viññū sabrahmacārī te pāpake akusale dhamme pahīne samanupassantī”ti.

translation_text.an10.87:1.1: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants concerning the mendicant Kalandaka:
translation_text.an10.87:2.1: “Firstly, a mendicant raises disciplinary issues and doesn’t praise the settlement of disciplinary issues.
translation_text.an10.87:2.2: This quality doesn’t conduce to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an10.87:3.1: Furthermore, a mendicant doesn’t want to train, and doesn’t praise taking up the training. …
translation_text.an10.87:4.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has corrupt wishes, and doesn’t praise getting rid of wishes. …
translation_text.an10.87:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is irritable, and doesn’t praise getting rid of anger. …
translation_text.an10.87:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant denigrates others, and doesn’t praise getting rid of denigration. …
translation_text.an10.87:7.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is devious, and doesn’t praise getting rid of deviousness. …
translation_text.an10.87:8.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is deceitful, and doesn’t praise getting rid of deceitfulness. …
translation_text.an10.87:9.1: Furthermore, a mendicant doesn’t pay attention to the teachings, and doesn’t praise attending to the teachings. …
translation_text.an10.87:10.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is not in retreat, and doesn’t praise retreat. …
translation_text.an10.87:11.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is inhospitable to their spiritual companions, and doesn’t praise hospitality.
translation_text.an10.87:11.2: This quality doesn’t conduce to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an10.87:12.1: Even though a mendicant such as this might wish:
translation_text.an10.87:12.2: ‘If only my spiritual companions would honor, respect, esteem, and venerate me!’ Still they don’t honor, respect, esteem, and venerate them.
translation_text.an10.87:12.4: Because their sensible spiritual companions see that they haven’t given up those bad unskillful qualities.
translation_text.an10.87:13.1: Suppose a wild colt was to wish:
translation_text.an10.87:13.2: ‘If only the humans would put me in a thoroughbred’s place, feed me a thoroughbred’s food, and give me a thoroughbred’s grooming.’ Still the humans wouldn’t put them in a thoroughbred’s place, feed them a thoroughbred’s food, or give them a thoroughbred’s grooming.
translation_text.an10.87:13.4: Because sensible humans see that they haven’t given up their tricks, bluffs, ruses, and feints.
translation_text.an10.87:13.5: In the same way, even though a mendicant such as this might wish:
translation_text.an10.87:13.6: ‘If only my spiritual companions would honor, respect, esteem, and venerate me!’ Still they don’t honor, respect, esteem, and venerate them.
translation_text.an10.87:13.8: Because their sensible spiritual companions see that they haven’t given up those bad unskillful qualities.
translation_text.an10.87:14.1: Next, a mendicant doesn’t raise disciplinary issues and praises the settlement of disciplinary issues.
translation_text.an10.87:14.2: This quality conduces to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an10.87:15.1: Furthermore, a mendicant wants to train, and praises taking up the training. …
translation_text.an10.87:16.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has few desires, and praises getting rid of desires. …
translation_text.an10.87:17.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is not irritable, and praises getting rid of anger. …
translation_text.an10.87:18.1: Furthermore, a mendicant doesn’t denigrate others, and praises getting rid of denigration. …
translation_text.an10.87:19.1: Furthermore, a mendicant isn’t devious, and praises getting rid of deviousness. …
translation_text.an10.87:20.1: Furthermore, a mendicant isn’t deceitful, and praises getting rid of deceitfulness. …
translation_text.an10.87:21.1: Furthermore, a mendicant pays attention to the teachings, and praises attending to the teachings. …
translation_text.an10.87:22.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is in retreat, and praises retreat. …
translation_text.an10.87:23.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is hospitable to their spiritual companions, and praises hospitality.
translation_text.an10.87:23.2: This quality conduces to fondness, respect, esteem, harmony, and unity.
translation_text.an10.87:24.1: Even though a mendicant such as this might never wish:
translation_text.an10.87:24.2: ‘If only my spiritual companions would honor, respect, esteem, and venerate me!’ Still they honor, respect, esteem, and venerate them.
translation_text.an10.87:24.4: Because their sensible spiritual companions see that they’ve given up those bad unskillful qualities.
translation_text.an10.87:25.1: Suppose a fine thoroughbred never wished:
translation_text.an10.87:25.2: ‘If only the humans would put me in a thoroughbred’s place, feed me a thoroughbred’s food, and give me a thoroughbred’s grooming.’ Still the humans would put them in a thoroughbred’s place, feed them a thoroughbred’s food, and give them a thoroughbred’s grooming.
translation_text.an10.87:25.4: Because sensible humans see that they’ve given up their tricks, bluffs, ruses, and feints.
translation_text.an10.87:26.1: In the same way, even though a mendicant such as this might never wish:
translation_text.an10.87:26.2: ‘If only my spiritual companions would honor, respect, esteem, and venerate me!’ Still they honor, respect, esteem, and venerate them.
translation_text.an10.87:26.4: Because their sensible spiritual companions see that they’ve given up those bad unskillful qualities.”

variant_text.an10.87:1.1: kālaṅkataṁ bhikkhuṁ → kalandakaṁ bhikkhuṁ (bj); kāḷakabhikkhuṁ (sya-all); kālakaṁ bhikkhuṁ (pts1ed)
variant_text.an10.87:12.2: garuṁ kareyyuṁ → garukareyyuṁ (bj, sya-all, pts1ed) | garuṁ karonti → garukaronti (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
